title: The Top 11 Herbal Creams for Calming Jaw Clenching and Stress

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valeranone is good for jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the body, reducing muscle tension and helping to relax the jaw muscles that are clenched.
Valerenic acid is good for jaw clenching because it can help relax muscles and reduce stress, which often cause jaw clenching and tension.

Valerenal has been found to be effective in reducing jaw clenching due to its sedative properties, which help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, thereby alleviating the symptoms of bruxism.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with jaw clenching. This type of cream is made from a plant called valerian, which is known for its calming properties. When you clench your jaw, it can cause pain and tension in the face and neck muscles. The valerian in the cream helps to relax these muscles and reduce the pain. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and ease any discomfort. Valeriana officinalis cream can be applied directly to the affected area, usually the jaw and temples, to help relieve tension and pain. By reducing the tension in the jaw muscles, the cream can also help to prevent further clenching and grinding of the teeth, which can lead to problems like headaches and earaches.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, you will need dried Salvia leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Salvia leaves with 8 ounces of carrier oil in a small saucepan, and heat it gently over low heat for about 20 minutes, or until the mixture reaches 160-180°F. Next, let the mixture infuse for at least 4 hours, or overnight, allowing the Salvia's properties to transfer into the oil. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 ounces of the infused oil over low heat, stirring constantly. Once melted, remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before pouring it into a small tin or jar. As the mixture cools, it will thicken into a creamy texture, ready to be applied to the jaw area to help relax and soothe the muscles.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for jaw clenching

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle tension and swelling in the jaw, thereby alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching such as pain and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for jaw clenching because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw, which can lead to a decrease in jaw clenching and grinding.
Kaempferol is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce tension and pain in the jaw muscles.
Tannins are good for jaw clenching because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw and facial muscles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream is often used to help people who clench their jaws, a condition known as bruxism. This cream works by affecting the brain's nervous system, which controls muscle movement. When you clench your jaw, it's often because of stress or anxiety. The cream helps calm the nervous system, making it easier to relax and stop clenching your jaw. Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and insomnia, which are common causes of jaw clenching. The cream is applied directly to the jaw area and is absorbed into the skin, helping to relax the muscles and reduce tension. By reducing stress and anxiety, the cream helps to break the cycle of jaw clenching, giving you a more relaxed and comfortable jaw.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, start by drying fresh or dried Salvia leaves and flowers. You'll need about 1 cup of these dried leaves and flowers. Next, combine the dried herbs with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Let this mixture infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Then, mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of the infused oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the wax is fully dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before pouring it into a clean glass jar or tin. Let the cream set at room temperature before using it. Apply the cream to your jaw area as needed, massaging it gently into the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for jaw clenching

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety that often cause people to clench their jaws.
Linalyl acetate is good for jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often lead to jaw clenching and tension.

Rosmarinic acid helps to reduce jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw area, making it a natural remedy for people who experience jaw clenching and grinding.
Lavandulol is good for jaw clenching because it has a calming effect that can help relax tense muscles in the jaw and face, reducing clenching and grinding of the teeth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia cream is a good remedy for jaw clenching due to its calming properties. This cream contains lavender oil, which is known to relax tense muscles. When we clench our jaw, the muscles in our face and neck become tight, causing pain and discomfort. Lavandula angustifolia cream helps to soothe these muscles, reducing tension and promoting relaxation. The lavender oil in the cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area. By applying the cream to the affected area, you can experience a sense of calm and relief from jaw clenching. The cooling sensation of the cream can also help to numb the pain, providing temporary relief. Regular use of Lavandula angustifolia cream may also help to reduce the frequency of jaw clenching, as it can help to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream for jaw clenching relief, start by infusing dried Salvia leaves in a carrier oil. Combine 1 cup of dried Salvia leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark spot for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1-2 tablespoons of a natural emollient like beeswax or shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring the mixture into small glass jars or tubes. Once the mixture has cooled and solidified, label the jars and store them in a cool, dark place. To use, simply apply a small amount of the cream to your jaw area and massage it in gently. The Salvia officinalis in the cream may help relax tense muscles and reduce jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for jaw clenching

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with jaw clenching. This is because it contains calming properties that can soothe muscle tension. When you clench your jaw, it puts strain on the masseter muscle, which can cause pain and discomfort. The Melissa officinalis cream works by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscle, making it easier to release the tension. It also has a cooling effect that can help numb the area and provide quick relief from pain. Additionally, Melissa officinalis cream is often combined with other natural ingredients, such as arnica or chamomile, which can enhance its calming effects and make it more effective at reducing jaw clenching. When applied topically, the cream can directly target the affected area and provide fast relief from pain and tension.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, start by harvesting or buying dried Salvia officinalis leaves. Dry the leaves completely by spreading them on a paper towel or cloth and letting them air out for a few days. Then, combine 1 tablespoon of the dried Salvia leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass bowl. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids and let the liquid cool down. Once cooled, mix the liquid with 1/2 cup of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a separate bowl, then stir in 1 tablespoon of beeswax or candelilla wax. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan with a heat diffuser until the wax is melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove from heat and let it cool and thicken before pouring it into a glass jar. Store the cream in a cool place and apply it to the affected area as needed to help relax the jaw muscles and reduce clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for jaw clenching

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you clench your jaw, it can cause pain and inflammation in the muscles. The ginger in the cream helps reduce this inflammation, making it easier to move your jaw. Ginger also has a warming effect, which can help relax the muscles and soothe the pain. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that help protect the muscles from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, applying Zingiber officinale cream to the affected area can help relieve tension and discomfort in the jaw and surrounding muscles. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, you will need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a cream base. First, steep the dried Salvia officinalis leaves in the carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat for about 30 minutes, or until the leaves have lost their color. Then, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, and discard the leaves. Next, melt the beeswax in a separate saucepan over low heat, and then mix it with the cream base and the infused oil in a separate bowl. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax has melted, and then pour the mixture into a glass jar. Let it cool and solidify completely before using it. To apply the cream, simply massage a small amount into the skin where you experience jaw clenching, and enjoy the relaxing effects of Salvia officinalis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams are helpful for jaw clenching because they contain a natural ingredient called Avena sativa extract, also known as oat kernel extract. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with jaw clenching, also known as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) pain, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. Begin by infusing the dried Salvia officinalis leaves in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily to help the herbs release their properties. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil and discard the leaves. In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt the beeswax and then slowly add the infused oil, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, add a few drops of vitamin E oil as a preservative and mix well. Transfer the cream to a clean container, label it, and store it in the refrigerator to prevent spoilage. Apply the cream to your jaw area as needed, massaging it gently to help relax the muscles and reduce tension.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, can be helpful for jaw clenching due to its cooling and relaxing properties. When you clench your jaw, it can cause pain and tension in the muscles of your face and neck. The menthol in peppermint creams can help to numb the area and reduce pain. It can also help to relax the muscles, making it easier to release the tension that causes jaw clenching. When you apply peppermint cream to your jaw and face, it can help to break the cycle of clenching and pain. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with jaw clenching, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, dry the Salvia officinalis leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on a baking sheet and placing them in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 1-2 hours. Once the leaves are completely dry, crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Next, combine the powdered Salvia leaves with a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a small saucepan and heat the mixture over low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. The heat will help to infuse the oil with the beneficial properties of the Salvia leaves. After the mixture has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove the solids. Finally, add a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to the oil mixture and stir until it has fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool until it has solidified into a rich, creamy balm that can be applied topically to help soothe and relax the jaw muscles.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, you will need some dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, infuse the dried Salvia officinalis leaves in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and leaving it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short bursts, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly until it's fully incorporated. remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a helpful treatment for jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. This condition causes people to involuntarily grind or clench their teeth, especially at night. This can lead to pain and tension in the jaw, face, and neck. Glycyrrhiza glabra cream contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties. When applied topically to the jaw area, glycyrrhizin can help reduce swelling and ease muscle tension. It does this by blocking the production of a hormone called aldosterone, which can contribute to muscle spasms and pain. As a result, people with jaw clenching may experience relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, the cream may also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for bruxism.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, you will need some dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil, a beeswax, and a small saucepan. First, steep a cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves in a cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a saucepan over low heat for about 30 minutes. Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the leaves. Next, in another saucepan, melt one tablespoon of beeswax over low heat. Once the beeswax is melted, add the Salvia-infused oil to it and stir well. Continue heating the mixture for a few more minutes until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before pouring it into a small container. Allow the cream to cool completely before using it. To use the cream, simply massage a small amount onto your jaw area, focusing on the areas where you tend to clench your jaw. The Salvia officinalis in the cream may help to relax your muscles and reduce tension in your jaw, providing relief from jaw clenching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including jaw clenching. The cream made from this herb is good for jaw clenching because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension. When we clench our jaw, the muscles in our face and jaw become tight and inflamed, which can cause pain and discomfort. The active ingredients in Salvia officinalis cream, such as camphor and salvenic acid, work to calm and soothe these muscles, reducing the inflammation and allowing the muscles to relax. This can help to decrease the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis cream can also help to reduce redness and swelling in the affected area, promoting a faster recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with jaw clenching, start by drying and preparing the herbs. Harvest fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, and dry them completely either by air-drying or using a dehydrator. Once dry, crush the leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of the dried Salvia powder with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties. Remove from heat, let it cool, and then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Now, mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a separate saucepan, and heat over low heat until the beeswax has melted. Combine the infused oil mixture with the beeswax-shea butter mixture, and stir until well combined. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar, and let it cool and solidify before using. Apply a small amount to the temples or jaw area to help relax and reduce jaw clenching.

intro_desc: Jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, can cause pain and discomfort in the jaw, face, and neck. Using herbal creams can provide relief from these symptoms. One key herb in these creams is Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root. Valerian root has a calming effect on the body, which can help to relax the muscles in the jaw and face, reducing the tension that leads to clenching. This herb is commonly used to treat anxiety and insomnia, but its calming properties can also be beneficial for jaw clenching.  Another herb used in these creams is Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower. Passionflower is known for its ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. It works by affecting the brain's neurotransmitters, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce the urge to clench the jaw. By using a cream containing these herbs, you can soothe the muscles in your jaw and face, reducing the pain and discomfort associated with bruxism.  Fixing the issue of jaw clenching is important because it can lead to more serious problems if left untreated. These problems include tooth damage, headaches, and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. TMJ disorders can cause pain and stiffness in the jaw, face, and neck, making everyday activities like eating and talking difficult. By addressing jaw clenching early on, you can prevent these problems and improve your overall quality of life. This can be achieved by practicing relaxation techniques, such as meditation or deep breathing, and using herbal creams to calm the muscles in your jaw and face.  Using herbal creams can also be a convenient and non-invasive way to manage jaw clenching. These creams can be applied directly to the jaw and face, providing fast and targeted relief from pain and discomfort. They can be used in combination with other relaxation techniques, such as exercise or cognitive behavioral therapy, to provide comprehensive relief from bruxism. By incorporating herbal creams into your daily routine, you can take the first steps towards managing jaw clenching and improving your overall well-being.
supplementary_best: To remedy jaw clenching, a combination of herbal creams containing Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, and Lavandula angustifolia is recommended. Valeriana officinalis has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw. Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, has sedative properties that soothe anxiety and promote relaxation, which can contribute to jaw clenching. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calm. The combination of these three herbs works well together because they address the physical and emotional aspects of jaw clenching. Valeriana officinalis and Passiflora incarnata help to relax the muscles, while Lavandula angustifolia helps to calm the mind, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the likelihood of jaw clenching.
supplementary_avoid: If you have jaw clenching, it's best to avoid herbal creams that contain herbs like 'Capsicum annuum' (cayenne pepper) and 'Echinacea purpurea' (coneflower). These herbs can irritate the skin and make jaw clenching worse. 'Capsicum annuum' contains capsaicin, a chemical that can cause blood vessels to dilate and increase blood flow to the skin. While this can help with pain, it can also make jaw clenching worse by stimulating the nerves in the face. 'Echinacea purpurea' has been shown to increase muscle contractions, which can make jaw clenching more severe. 'Cinchona officinalialis' (cinchona bark) is also not recommended, as it can increase heart rate and blood pressure, which can make jaw clenching worse. 'Eucalyptus globulus' (eucalyptus) may also be a problem, as it can stimulate the nerves in the face and make jaw clenching worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Massage therapy can help with jaw clenching by relaxing the muscles in the face and jaw. When you massage these areas, it increases blood flow and reduces tension, which in turn eases the clenching. This is because massage helps to break down adhesions and scar tissue in the muscles, allowing them to move more freely and reducing pain.  Aromatherapy can also be beneficial, as certain scents such as lavender and peppermint have a calming effect on the body. When you inhale these scents, they can help to relax the muscles and reduce stress, which is often a contributor to jaw clenching. Additionally, inhaling these scents can help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to relax the jaw and facial muscles.  Heat and cold therapy can also be used to help with jaw clenching. Applying heat or cold packs to the jaw and facial area can help to relax the muscles and reduce pain. Heat therapy, such as a warm bath or shower, can help to relax the muscles and increase blood flow, while cold therapy, such as an ice pack or cold compress, can help to numb the area and reduce pain.  Yoga and meditation can also be helpful in managing jaw clenching. These practices can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to jaw clenching. By practicing yoga and meditation, you can learn to relax the muscles and reduce tension, making it easier to manage jaw clenching. This is because these practices can help to calm the mind and body, reducing the physical symptoms of jaw clenching.
